ddls:
CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "first_name" TEXT /* First name of the user */,
  "last_name" TEXT /* Last name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "ccn" TEXT /* Encrypted credit card number */,
  "username" TEXT /* Unique username for the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Encrypted password for the user */,
  "date_of_birth" TEXT /* Date of birth of the user */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the user */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the user */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country of the user */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for when the user account was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for the last update to the user account */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Boolean to indicate if the user account is active */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp for the last successful login */,
  "profile_picture" TEXT /* URL or path to the user's profile picture */,
  "referral_code" TEXT /* Referral code for marketing purposes */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for when the role was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for the last update to the role */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Boolean to indicate if the role is active */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* JSONB field to store detailed permissions associated with the role */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "user_role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user-role association */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for when the user-role association was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for the last update to the user-role association */,
  "is_active" INTEGER /* Boolean to indicate if the association is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_role_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_roles_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES roles ("role_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_roles_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "login_attempts" (
  "attempt_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each login attempt */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user attempting to log in */,
  "attempt_time" TEXT /* Time of the login attempt */,
  "success" INTEGER /* Whether the login attempt was successful */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the login attempt was made */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string for additional context */,
  "location" TEXT /* Geolocation of the login attempt */,
  "lockout" INTEGER /* Boolean to indicate if the login attempt resulted in an account lockout */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("attempt_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_login_attempts_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "password_resets" (
  "reset_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each password reset request */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user requesting the password reset */,
  "request_time" TEXT /* Time the password reset was requested */,
  "token" TEXT /* Unique token used for the password reset */,
  "used" INTEGER /* Whether the token has been used */,
  "expires_at" TEXT /* Timestamp for when the password reset token expires */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the reset request was made */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string for additional context */,
  "location" TEXT /* Geolocation of the reset request */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("reset_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_password_resets_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "two_factor_auth" (
  "auth_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each two-factor authentication record */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "method" TEXT /* Method of two-factor authentication */,
  "enabled" INTEGER /* Whether two-factor authentication is enabled */,
  "last_verified" TEXT /* Time the two-factor authentication was last verified */,
  "secret_key" TEXT /* Secret key used for two-factor authentication */,
  "backup_codes" TEXT /* JSONB field to store backup codes for two-factor authentication */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the two-factor authentication was verified */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string for additional context */,
  "location" TEXT /* Geolocation of the verification */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("auth_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_two_factor_auth_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "activity_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each activity log */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user performing the activity */,
  "activity_time" TEXT /* Time of the activity */,
  "activity_type" TEXT /* Type of activity performed */,
  "details" TEXT /* Detailed description of the activity */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the activity was performed */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string for additional context */,
  "location" TEXT /* Geolocation of the activity */,
  "activity_details" TEXT /* JSONB field to store detailed information about the activity */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_activity_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "password_strength" (
  "strength_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each password strength record */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Encrypted password of the user */,
  "strength_score" INTEGER /* Score indicating the strength of the password */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Time when the password strength was last evaluated */,
  "rules_applied" TEXT /* JSONB field to store the rules applied to evaluate the password strength */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the password was last updated */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string for additional context */,
  "location" TEXT /* Geolocation of the password update */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("strength_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_password_strength_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "account_lockouts" (
  "lockout_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each account lockout */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user whose account is locked */,
  "lockout_time" TEXT /* Time when the account was locked */,
  "unlock_time" TEXT /* Time when the account will be unlocked */,
  "reason" TEXT /* Reason for the account lockout */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the lockout was triggered */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string for additional context */,
  "location" TEXT /* Geolocation of the lockout */,
  "lockout_details" TEXT /* JSONB field to store detailed information about the lockout */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("lockout_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_account_lockouts_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
